rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the component type scope when contracts relate to component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in the component implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Preserve the AADL architecture by not using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers from the current component or its ports in AGREE expressions
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions; use exposed ports or constants instead
Align helper declaration types with the expression types to prevent type mismatches
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Write AGREE contracts inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
